title: Connections, Channels, Heartbeats, Errors, and Recovery · Independent Type Trial
summary: An independent integration team receives different traffic, tenant, topology, schema, and failure constraints and transfers connections, channels, heartbeats, errors, and recovery into a new connection, channel, heartbeat, and recovery state machine. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of connection, channel, heartbeat, and recovery state machine with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causa…
objectives: Use bounded long-lived AMQP connections and explain multiplexed channels, negotiated limits, names, TLS, and lifecycle cost.
Assign channel ownership by publisher or consumer lifecycle and prevent concurrent confirm, acknowledgement, and topology operations from corrupting assumptions.
Choose a heartbeat timeout with network and flow-control evidence and distinguish heartbeat interval, TCP keepalive, and false-positive failure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Long Lived Connection
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab messaging team handles case 4515 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound heartbeat and reconnect policy while rejecting stale channel-generation callbacks; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement decision owner; then remove the dead-letter binding and expose the resulting retention risk. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Long Lived Connection.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use bounded long-lived amqp connections and explain multiplexed channels, negotiated limits, names, tls, and lifecycle cost. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Long Lived Connection, keep Long Lived Connection connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Recreate connection, channels, topology, confirm state, prefetch, consumers, and publisher admission in generation order while rejecting stale callbacks.
